input: A 2-year-old female child, third in birth order, born to parents who are first cousins, delivered vaginally at term without complications, presented with sparse scalp hair, absent body hair, eyebrows, and eyelashes, and delayed tooth eruption. The mother reported that the child does not sweat and has had recurrent fevers since the neonatal period. Psychomotor development was normal. There was no family history of similar symptoms, except for a previous female sibling with similar complaints and a distinctive appearance, who experienced recurrent fever and chest infections during the neonatal period and died at 6 months of age. The parents' first pregnancy ended in a miscarriage at 2 months for unknown reasons. Skin examination showed smooth, normal-looking skin with normal palmoplantar skin and dermatoglyphics. The child had an aged appearance with finely wrinkled skin around the eyes, an everted lower lip, and low-set ears. Scalp hair was sparse, light-colored, and thin, with complete absence of eyelashes, eyebrows, and body hair. Oral examination revealed only two conical, widely spaced maxillary teeth and a low-arched, flat palate. Nails were normal. Systemic examination showed no abnormalities, and there was no evidence of mental retardation. Imaging of the jaw showed a lack of teeth with only two maxillary teeth and a left mandibular tooth bud. Biochemical tests were normal.
